extraction.one_line_summary: SAGAN Peter won the bunch sprint after CAVAGNA Rémi's late attack was shut down by sprinter teams unwilling to give him any leeway with 7km remaining.
tactical_patterns: CAVAGNA Rémi immediately marked by sprinter teams despite only 7-second gap, showing reputation now prevents breakaway tolerance pre-sprint finishes.
SAGAN Peter positioned top-five wheels at 300m, refused following leadout attempts, relied on positioning over power conservation through roundabouts.
MOLANO Juan Sebastián chose SOTO's wheel sixth position while SAGAN took IMPEY fourth wheel, demonstrating positioning beats pure speed advantage.
Quick-Step attempted roundabout attacks at 700m and 500m markers but isolated their own leadout, revealing coordination failure pattern.

rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: First bunch sprint win in nearly two years despite fatigue from Tirreno-Sanremo and no planned attendance here.
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: positive: Strong condition attacking final climb but sprinter teams now categorically refuse any gap tolerance regardless of terrain.
rider_signals.MOLANO Juan Sebastián: negative: Picked wrong wheel sixth position while SAGAN positioned fourth, suggesting tactical positioning deficit cost potential victory.
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: positive: Exceeded GC expectations post-crash, with Bora-Hansgrohe controlling for sprint while maintaining GC position.
rider_signals.IMPEY Daryl: positive: Positioned third wheel with only one leadout man, kicked early 180m uphill, only beaten by SAGAN's superior positioning.
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: neutral: Pulled off leadout early in final 3km, leaving Quick-Step isolated but keeping YATES Adam safe from crashes.
